# Agentic SDLC Workshop — Copilot Instructions

## Purpose
This repository is an Agentic SDLC Workshop framework. It uses a
sequence of specialised Copilot agents to demonstrate AI-assisted
software development lifecycle — from business requirement through
to tested, working code.

Read `README.md` for the full framework overview before doing anything.

---

## Global Principles — Apply to Every Agent and Every Task

### Domain Fidelity
- Always read `docs/requirements/BRD.md` before producing any output.
- Preserve every entity name, role name, and lifecycle state verbatim
  from the BRD. Never rename, generalise, or substitute domain terms.
- If the BRD says `Appointment`, every downstream file says `Appointment`
  — not `Booking`, `Event`, or `Request`.

### Skill First
- Before producing any output, read the relevant skill file in
  `.github/skills/{skill-name}/SKILL.md`.
- The skill file is the authoritative instruction set for that task.
- Never skip the skill file — it contains guardrails, format rules,
  and examples that must be followed.

### No Clarifying Questions
- Never ask clarifying questions.
- Make reasonable assumptions and document them.
- If something is unclear, apply the most conservative interpretation
  and note the assumption in the output.

### No Premature Estimation
- Never add effort estimates during the work breakdown phase
  (user-story-agent).

### Traceability
- Every issue must trace back to a BRD functional requirement (FR-XXX).
- Never design, create, or implement something not present in the BRD.
- Use FR IDs to annotate decisions in design documents and issues.

### Tech Stack Agnostic
- Never prescribe specific frameworks, ORMs, libraries, or tools
  in any design document or issue.
- Describe patterns and contracts, not implementations.
- The design document describes what the system does — not how it
  is built.

### File Paths — Always Follow the Skill
- Always save files to the exact paths specified in the skill file.
- Never invent alternative paths or folder structures.
- If a folder does not exist, create it before saving.

### Idempotency
- Always check whether a file already exists before creating it.
- If it exists, overwrite it — never create numbered duplicates.

---

## Workspace Folder Structure

```
.github/
  agents/           Agent definition files (.agent.md)
  skills/           Skill instruction files (SKILL.md per folder)

docs/
  requirements/
    BRD.md          Generated by brd-agent
  design/
    design-doc.md   Generated by design-agent
  
  test-reports/                   Generated by playwright-agent (HTML)
  
  playwright-mcp-setup.md         Playwright MCP configuration guide

issues/             issue files generated by user-story-agent
e2e/                Playwright test files generated by playwright-agent
src/                Project scaffold generated by scaffold-agent;
                    feature code generated by implement-agent
```

---

## Agent Sequence — Always Follow This Order

```
1.  @brd-agent              → docs/requirements/BRD.md
2.  @design-agent           → docs/design/design-doc.md
3.  @scaffold-agent         → src/ (folder structure + entry points only)
4.  @user-story-agent       → issues/
5.  @push-issues-to-GitHub-agent  → creates issues in GitHub (github.com)
6.  @implement-agent        → src/ (DATABASE → BACKEND → FRONTEND → E2E-TEST)
7.  @playwright-agent       → e2e/*.spec.ts + docs/test-reports/


Never run an agent before its predecessors in the sequence have
completed and reviewed.

### On-Demand Agents — Available at Any Time

**`@review-agent`** — IDE code review agent. Invoke it in the IDE at any
point during or after step 7 to review implemented code against the
originating task's acceptance criteria. It reads `workshop-stack.md` to
apply the correct language and framework standards. It does not belong in
the numbered sequence — use it as a quality gate whenever you want a
structured review of a task implementation before moving to the next one.

**`@unit-test-agent`** — generates unit tests for implemented `[BACKEND]` issues.
Invoke it after each `[BACKEND]` issue is implemented. It reads the BACKEND
issue's acceptance criteria and the implemented code to derive test cases.

---

## Issue Hierarchy

```
Epic → Feature → User Story → Task
```

- Epics represent business capabilities — not technical layers
- Features are independently demonstrable slices of an epic
- User stories are written from the user's perspective using
  role names from the BRD
- Tasks are typed implementation units: DATABASE, BACKEND, FRONTEND, E2E-TEST
- Task implementation order is always: DATABASE → BACKEND → FRONTEND → E2E-TEST

---

## HTML Reports
- All HTML reports must be self-contained — no external CSS or JS
- Must render correctly in Chrome, Edge, and Safari
- No JavaScript required — use HTML and CSS only
- Font: system font stack — no external font imports
- All styles in a `<style>` block in the file

---

## Playwright E2E Tests
- Always read `docs/playwright-mcp-setup.md` before using Playwright MCP tools
- Read `playwright.config.ts` if it exists to confirm `testDir` and `baseURL`;
  if absent, derive `baseURL` from `dev_server_url` in `workshop-stack.md`
- Always use `data-testid` selectors — never CSS classes or tag selectors
- `data-testid` values must come from the design document — never invent them
- Save all test files to the `e2e_tests_folder` defined in `workshop-stack.md`
- Tests run against the application at `baseURL` — confirm it is running first
- The HTML test report is written to `docs/test-reports/`

---

## Implementation Conventions — Applied When Reading workshop-stack.md

These rules apply to implement-agent, scaffold-agent, unit-test-agent, and review-agent
whenever they read `workshop-stack.md`.

### Backend
- All API routes are prefixed with `/api/`
- All protected routes use the authentication middleware defined in `auth_middleware`
- All error responses use the shape defined by `api_error_format` (if present)
- Async/concurrency model follows the convention of the chosen language and framework
  (e.g. async/await for Node.js and Python, CompletableFuture for Java, async/await for C#)
- Avoid language-specific anti-patterns (e.g. no bare `except` in Python,
  no raw SQL strings without parameterisation in any language)

### Database
- `schema_file`: use for single-file ORMs (e.g. Prisma). Omit for per-class ORMs or NoSQL.
- `entities_folder`: use for per-class ORMs (JPA, EF Core, SQLAlchemy). Omit for single-file ORMs.
- `migrations_folder`: relational databases only (PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, SQL Server, Oracle).
  Omit for NoSQL / schema-less databases (MongoDB, Cosmos DB, DynamoDB, Firestore).
- `seed_file`: required for all database types. Format depends on the database.
- Never modify pre-existing models marked `// PRE-BUILT`. Mark new models `// NEW`.
- All categorical fields use the ORM's enumerated type — never plain strings.

### Frontend
- Follow the component model of the chosen framework
  (functional components + hooks for React, Composition API for Vue,
  component classes for Angular, code-behind for Blazor)
- All interactive elements must have `data-testid` attributes
- `data-testid` values must match exactly what is defined in the design document
- API calls go in `services_folder` — never inline in components
- Use existing auth context or session — do not rebuild authentication

### Testing
- Unit tests cover API endpoints and business logic
- E2E tests use `data-testid` selectors from the design document
- E2E tests cover the happy path and at least one error scenario
- Never test pre-built auth functionality
- E2E test files are always TypeScript (`.spec.ts`) regardless of backend language

---

## Never
- Rename domain entities defined in the BRD
- Add effort estimates during work breakdown phases
- Prescribe specific technologies in design documents
- Create issues not traceable to a BRD functional requirement
- Skip reading the skill file before producing output
- Create duplicate files — always overwrite on re-run
- Use CSS class, tag, or positional selectors in Playwright tests
- Invent `data-testid` values not defined in the design document
- Run Playwright MCP tools without confirming the application is running
